{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NJ9fmCTZCZyXDnGf2jhC3q","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"2bde1117-144a-4515-a646-883e9e0b61f1","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:00:11.016Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01P76wauzH8VXEeE93d66iNf","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    33→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    34→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    35→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    36→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    37→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    38→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    39→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    40→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    41→\n    42→## Deployment\n    43→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    44→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    45→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    46→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    47→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    48→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    49→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    50→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    51→\n    52→## React + SQLite\n    53→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    54→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    55→\n    56→## Security\n    57→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    58→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    59→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    60→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    61→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    62→\n    63→## Working Directory\n    64→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    65→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    66→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    67→\n    68→## Security\n    69→- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n    70→- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n    71→- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n    72→- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n    73→- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n    74→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"89f7a469-a09e-48b5-aff2-f9937628d3ae","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:00:11.405Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n\n## Security\n- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n","numLines":74,"startLine":1,"totalLines":74}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013KMJqxgiMepsqiFm6wtDdR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-24 10:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 13: found and fixed 3 bugs in PATCH endpoint and application lifecycle. Created issue #100, implemented fixes in PR #101, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 201 tests pass\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main\n     9→- **Open PRs**: None\n    10→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    11→- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n    12→\n    13→## Work Completed\n    14→\n    15→### Issue #100: Code review round 13 (PR #101 — merged + deployed)\n    16→\n    17→3 fixes:\n    18→\n    19→1. **`is_full` never set to 1 when `maxGroupSize` reduced to equal member count** (CRITICAL) — The PATCH handler only checked `< currentMemberCount` (reject) and `> currentMemberCount && is_full` (reopen), but missed the `=== currentMemberCount` case. When an author reduced group size to exactly the current count, `is_full` stayed 0, leaving the listing visible in browse and appearing joinable. Added the missing equality branch.\n    20→\n    21→2. **Transaction mutates outer `updates`/`values` arrays** (CRITICAL) — The `updates` and `values` arrays were declared outside the `db.transaction()` closure but mutated inside it via `.push()`. Structurally unsafe — any retry or double-call would corrupt the SQL with duplicate bindings. Fixed by creating local copies (`txUpdates`/`txValues`) inside the transaction.\n    22→\n    23→3. **Users permanently locked out when `requires_approval` toggled off** (IMPORTANT) — When an author toggled `requires_approval` from true to false, users with pending applications became stuck: invisible to the author (gated by `requires_approval` check), blocked from direct join (pending app check), and blocked from re-apply (flag check). Fixed by auto-rejecting pending applications in the PATCH transaction when `requires_approval` is toggled off, and showing pending applicants to the author regardless of the flag.\n    24→\n    25→All 201 tests pass (3 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n    26→\n    27→## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n    28→- `notifyGroupFull` reads `platform_preference` outside the join transaction\n    29→- `BOT_TOKEN` / `CLIENT_ID` captured at module load time\n    30→- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints\n    31→- `requireAuth` does not re-validate user existence in DB (low risk — no admin deletion UI)\n    32→\n    33→## Issues Monitored\n    34→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human\n    35→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    36→\n    37→## Next Session Priority\n    38→1. Check production health\n    39→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    40→3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n    41→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"82c16b5b-8e37-4a34-8c73-3ed47cd1aeb3","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:00:21.410Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-24-1000.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-24 10:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 13: found and fixed 3 bugs in PATCH endpoint and application lifecycle. Created issue #100, implemented fixes in PR #101, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 201 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #100: Code review round 13 (PR #101 — merged + deployed)\n\n3 fixes:\n\n1. **`is_full` never set to 1 when `maxGroupSize` reduced to equal member count** (CRITICAL) — The PATCH handler only checked `< currentMemberCount` (reject) and `> currentMemberCount && is_full` (reopen), but missed the `=== currentMemberCount` case. When an author reduced group size to exactly the current count, `is_full` stayed 0, leaving the listing visible in browse and appearing joinable. Added the missing equality branch.\n\n2. **Transaction mutates outer `updates`/`values` arrays** (CRITICAL) — The `updates` and `values` arrays were declared outside the `db.transaction()` closure but mutated inside it via `.push()`. Structurally unsafe — any retry or double-call would corrupt the SQL with duplicate bindings. Fixed by creating local copies (`txUpdates`/`txValues`) inside the transaction.\n\n3. **Users permanently locked out when `requires_approval` toggled off** (IMPORTANT) — When an author toggled `requires_approval` from true to false, users with pending applications became stuck: invisible to the author (gated by `requires_approval` check), blocked from direct join (pending app check), and blocked from re-apply (flag check). Fixed by auto-rejecting pending applications in the PATCH transaction when `requires_approval` is toggled off, and showing pending applicants to the author regardless of the flag.\n\nAll 201 tests pass (3 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Also Noted (not fixed this round)\n- `notifyGroupFull` reads `platform_preference` outside the join transaction\n- `BOT_TOKEN` / `CLIENT_ID` captured at module load time\n- No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints\n- `requireAuth` does not re-validate user existence in DB (low risk — no admin deletion UI)\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n","numLines":41,"startLine":1,"totalLines":41}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TzoVmYZVpDWmdiM7JaePth","type":"tool_result","content":"title:\tCode review round 13: PATCH transaction bugs, orphaned applications\nstate:\tOPEN\nauthor:\tTommyBrennan\nlabels:\tapproved, bug\ncomments:\t0\nassignees:\t\nprojects:\tBookmate (Todo)\nmilestone:\t\nnumber:\t100\n--\n## Findings from code review round 13\n\n### 1. CRITICAL: PATCH transaction mutates outer arrays (structurally unsafe)\n**File:** `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` lines 214-275\n\nThe `updates` and `values` arrays are declared outside the `db.transaction()` closure but mutated inside it. While better-sqlite3 doesn't auto-retry, this pattern is fragile — if the transaction function were ever called more than once, the SQL statement would be corrupted with duplicate bindings.\n\n### 2. CRITICAL: `is_full` never set to 1 when `maxGroupSize` reduced to equal member count\n**File:** `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` lines 264-271\n\nWhen an author reduces `max_group_size` to exactly the current member count, `is_full` stays at 0. The listing remains visible in browse and appears joinable (though the join transaction rejects). Stale `is_full` flag.\n\n### 3. IMPORTANT: Users with pending apps become permanently locked out when `requires_approval` toggled off\n**Files:** `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` line 77, `join/route.ts` lines 56-68, `apply/route.ts` line 36\n\nWhen `requires_approval` switches from true to false:\n- Author can't see pending applicants (gated by `requires_approval` check)\n- Users can't join directly (blocked by pending app check)  \n- Users can't re-apply (blocked by `!requires_approval` check)\n- Users are stuck in limbo permanently","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"3e6d9a32-cfb2-4608-b841-0a3e758da2ee","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:00:23.217Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"title:\tCode review round 13: PATCH transaction bugs, orphaned applications\nstate:\tOPEN\nauthor:\tTommyBrennan\nlabels:\tapproved, bug\ncomments:\t0\nassignees:\t\nprojects:\tBookmate (Todo)\nmilestone:\t\nnumber:\t100\n--\n## Findings from code review round 13\n\n### 1. CRITICAL: PATCH transaction mutates outer arrays (structurally unsafe)\n**File:** `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` lines 214-275\n\nThe `updates` and `values` arrays are declared outside the `db.transaction()` closure but mutated inside it. While better-sqlite3 doesn't auto-retry, this pattern is fragile — if the transaction function were ever called more than once, the SQL statement would be corrupted with duplicate bindings.\n\n### 2. CRITICAL: `is_full` never set to 1 when `maxGroupSize` reduced to equal member count\n**File:** `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` lines 264-271\n\nWhen an author reduces `max_group_size` to exactly the current member count, `is_full` stays at 0. The listing remains visible in browse and appears joinable (though the join transaction rejects). Stale `is_full` flag.\n\n### 3. IMPORTANT: Users with pending apps become permanently locked out when `requires_approval` toggled off\n**Files:** `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` line 77, `join/route.ts` lines 56-68, `apply/route.ts` line 36\n\nWhen `requires_approval` switches from true to false:\n- Author can't see pending applicants (gated by `requires_approval` check)\n- Users can't join directly (blocked by pending app check)  \n- Users can't re-apply (blocked by `!requires_approval` check)\n- Users are stuck in limbo permanently","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01QgDCkfmpvYytm1xPhSs1gx","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","name":"Agent","input":{"subagent_type":"feature-dev:code-reviewer","description":"Code review round 14","prompt":"Do a thorough code review of the Bookmate Next.js application at /root/Projects/bookmate/app. This is a reading group matchmaking web app using Next.js 15, SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session, and Tailwind CSS.\n\nFocus areas (these were noted but not fixed in previous reviews):\n1. `notifyGroupFull` reads `platform_preference` outside the join transaction — potential stale data\n2. `BOT_TOKEN` / `CLIENT_ID` captured at module load time — won't pick up env changes\n3. No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints — orphaned data on user/listing deletion\n4. `requireAuth` does not re-validate user existence in DB\n\nAlso look for NEW issues in these files:\n- app/api/ — all API routes\n- app/lib/ — core libs (db, session, notifications)\n- app/components/ — React components\n- app/app/ — pages\n\nFocus on:\n- Race conditions and concurrency bugs\n- Security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, XSS, auth bypass)\n- Data integrity issues\n- Error handling gaps\n- Logic errors\n- TypeScript type safety issues\n\nDo NOT report:\n- Style/formatting issues\n- Missing features\n- Performance optimizations (unless critical)\n- Issues already fixed in recent commits\n\nFor each bug found, report: severity (CRITICAL/IMPORTANT/MINOR), file path, line numbers, what's wrong, and how to fix it."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":448,"cache_read_input_tokens":22035,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":448},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"f9371dc2-aa53-45cc-bf5e-62a98f1b8bd5"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MPqa32aH86572CmntBRZcg","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→\n    76→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    77→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    78→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    83→    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    84→    decided_at TEXT,\n    85→    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    86→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    87→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    88→  );\n    89→`);\n    90→\n    91→// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval column\n    92→try {\n    93→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n    94→} catch {\n    95→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→// Idempotent migration: add platform_preference column\n    99→try {\n   100→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram'\");\n   101→} catch {\n   102→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→// Idempotent migration: add discord_link column\n   106→try {\n   107→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_link TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   108→} catch {\n   109→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   110→}\n   111→\n   112→// Idempotent migration: add discord_channel_id column\n   113→try {\n   114→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   115→} catch {\n   116→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→// Idempotent migration: add telegram_chat_id column for auto-telegram feature\n   120→try {\n   121→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN telegram_chat_id INTEGER\");\n   122→} catch {\n   123→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   124→}\n   125→\n   126→// Tables for Telegram bot integration\n   127→db.exec(`\n   128→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n   129→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   130→    telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   131→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   132→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id),\n   133→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n   134→  );\n   135→\n   136→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n   137→    chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n   138→    chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   139→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n   140→  );\n   141→\n   142→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_user_links (\n   143→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   144→    telegram_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   145→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   146→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id),\n   147→    UNIQUE (telegram_user_id),\n   148→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   149→  );\n   150→`);\n   151→\n   152→// Rating and Reputation system\n   153→db.exec(`\n   154→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n   155→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n   156→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   157→    rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   158→    rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   159→    score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n   160→    comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   161→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   162→    UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n   163→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n   164→    FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n   165→    FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   166→  );\n   167→`);\n   168→\n   169→// Performance indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\n   170→db.exec(`\n   171→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\n   172→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\n   173→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\n   174→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n   175→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_applications_listing_status ON listing_applications(listing_id, status);\n   176→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_unread ON notifications(user_id, is_read);\n   177→  CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pending_telegram_chat_id ON pending_telegram_groups(telegram_chat_id);\n   178→`);\n   179→\n   180→export default db;\n   181→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"ae9550d8-0862-4169-9967-87eb2f0ee406","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:01:14.414Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01STBZ18tTeyhCes8c7EVzWi","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→import { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n     3→import { escapeHtmlForEmail } from \"./crypto-utils\";\n     4→\n     5→function getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n     6→  const user = db\n     7→    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n     8→    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n     9→  return user?.email || null;\n    10→}\n    11→\n    12→export function createNotification(\n    13→  userId: number,\n    14→  listingId: number | null,\n    15→  type: string,\n    16→  message: string\n    17→) {\n    18→  db.prepare(\n    19→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    20→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    21→\n    22→  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n    23→  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n    24→  if (email) {\n    25→    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    26→    // HTML-escape the message to prevent injection via user-controlled content\n    27→    // (display names, book titles) embedded in notification messages\n    28→    const htmlBody = `<p>${escapeHtmlForEmail(message)}</p>`;\n    29→    sendEmail(email, subject, message, htmlBody).catch(() => {\n    30→      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    31→    });\n    32→  }\n    33→}\n    34→\n    35→function getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n    36→  switch (type) {\n    37→    case \"new_member\":\n    38→      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    39→    case \"group_full\":\n    40→      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    41→    case \"application_received\":\n    42→      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    43→    case \"application_approved\":\n    44→      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    45→    case \"application_rejected\":\n    46→      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    47→    case \"rating_received\":\n    48→      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    49→    case \"listing_deleted\":\n    50→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been cancelled\";\n    51→    case \"listing_updated\":\n    52→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been updated\";\n    53→    case \"telegram_ready\":\n    54→      return \"Bookmate: Your Telegram reading group is ready!\";\n    55→    case \"discord_ready\":\n    56→      return \"Bookmate: Your Discord channel is ready!\";\n    57→    default:\n    58→      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    59→  }\n    60→}\n    61→\n    62→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    63→  listingId: number,\n    64→  joinerName: string\n    65→) {\n    66→  const listing = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    69→    )\n    70→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    71→\n    72→  if (listing) {\n    73→    createNotification(\n    74→      listing.author_id,\n    75→      listingId,\n    76→      \"new_member\",\n    77→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    78→    );\n    79→  }\n    80→}\n    81→\n    82→export function notifyApplicationReceived(\n    83→  listingId: number,\n    84→  applicantName: string\n    85→) {\n    86→  const listing = db\n    87→    .prepare(\n    88→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    89→    )\n    90→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    91→\n    92→  if (listing) {\n    93→    createNotification(\n    94→      listing.author_id,\n    95→      listingId,\n    96→      \"application_received\",\n    97→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    98→    );\n    99→  }\n   100→}\n   101→\n   102→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n   103→  userId: number,\n   104→  listingId: number,\n   105→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n   106→) {\n   107→  const listing = db\n   108→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   109→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   110→\n   111→  if (listing) {\n   112→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   113→    const message =\n   114→      decision === \"approved\"\n   115→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   116→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   117→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   118→  }\n   119→}\n   120→\n   121→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   122→  const listing = db\n   123→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference, author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   124→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string; platform_preference: string; author_id: number } | undefined;\n   125→  // Include all members (including the author) — author needs to know group is full to create the chat\n   126→  const members = db\n   127→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   128→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   129→\n   130→  if (listing) {\n   131→    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n   132→    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n   133→      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n   134→      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   135→    const message = botConfigured\n   136→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n   137→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n   138→\n   139→    for (const member of members) {\n   140→      createNotification(\n   141→        member.user_id,\n   142→        listingId,\n   143→        \"group_full\",\n   144→        message\n   145→      );\n   146→    }\n   147→  }\n   148→}\n   149→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"bb77ac38-719c-438c-ae13-339ff233fd07","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:01:14.829Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SnxPtgffX7WNgnEUpg1vtA","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→\n     4→export interface SessionData {\n     5→  userId?: number;\n     6→  email?: string;\n     7→  displayName?: string;\n     8→}\n     9→\n    10→const DEV_SECRET =\n    11→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    12→\n    13→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    14→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    15→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    16→    throw new Error(\n    17→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→  return {\n    21→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    22→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    23→    cookieOptions: {\n    24→      httpOnly: true,\n    25→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n    26→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    27→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    28→    },\n    29→  };\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→export async function getSession() {\n    33→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    34→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    35→}\n    36→\n    37→export async function requireAuth() {\n    38→  const session = await getSession();\n    39→  if (!session.userId) {\n    40→    return null;\n    41→  }\n    42→  return session;\n    43→}\n    44→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"28727054-f11b-43fb-82a8-df8aa525498a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:01:14.837Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a624d29c4716e91ef","tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","description":"Reading app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":16612,"tool_uses":6,"duration_ms":10133},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"8320d9f9-b65f-4bc9-b349-8e941e7770d3","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01RviEe1NNkP2NowpJChyxHQ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01SsdY1aduLUZExdsaLxwj4L","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4878,"cache_read_input_tokens":11541,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4878,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"1d8a6c32-0033-41fc-9e57-4eb6184e32ea"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SsdY1aduLUZExdsaLxwj4L","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n    30→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    42→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Check if already a member\n    46→  const existing = db\n    47→    .prepare(\n    48→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    )\n    50→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    51→\n    52→  if (existing) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Check if user has a pending application (e.g., requires_approval was toggled off)\n    57→  const pendingApp = db\n    58→    .prepare(\n    59→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    60→    )\n    61→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    62→\n    63→  if (pendingApp) {\n    64→    return NextResponse.json(\n    65→      { error: \"You already have a pending application for this group\" },\n    66→      { status: 400 }\n    67→    );\n    68→  }\n    69→\n    70→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    71→  // Re-read max_group_size inside the transaction to avoid stale data from concurrent PATCH\n    72→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    73→    const freshListing = db\n    74→      .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    75→      .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\n    76→\n    77→    if (!freshListing) {\n    78→      return { error: \"Listing not found\", count: 0, maxSize: 0 };\n    79→    }\n    80→\n    81→    const { count } = db\n    82→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    83→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    84→\n    85→    if (count >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    86→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count, maxSize: freshListing.max_group_size };\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    db.prepare(\n    90→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    91→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    92→\n    93→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    94→    if (newCount >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    95→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    96→    }\n    97→\n    98→    return { error: null, count: newCount, maxSize: freshListing.max_group_size };\n    99→  });\n   100→\n   101→  const result = joinTransaction();\n   102→\n   103→  if (result.error) {\n   104→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n   105→  }\n   106→\n   107→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n   108→  // When group just filled, skip new_member notification for the author\n   109→  // since they'll receive the more important group_full notification instead\n   110→  if (result.count === result.maxSize) {\n   111→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n   112→  } else {\n   113→    notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   114→  }\n   115→\n   116→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n   117→}\n   118→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"d4cf35d3-bce6-4c45-82f8-b8df506f0fc1","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:01:17.801Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CnyEWKMU9v4zgZUmzoTYGC","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationReceived } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json(\n    31→      { error: \"You cannot apply to your own listing\" },\n    32→      { status: 400 }\n    33→    );\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (!listing.requires_approval) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"This group does not require approval. Join directly.\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    44→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    45→  }\n    46→\n    47→  // Wrap membership check + application in a transaction to prevent TOCTOU races\n    48→  const applyTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    49→    // Check if already a member\n    50→    const isMember = db\n    51→      .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    52→      .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    53→\n    54→    if (isMember) {\n    55→      return { error: \"You are already a member\", status: 400 };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    // Re-check is_full inside transaction\n    59→    const currentListing = db\n    60→      .prepare(\"SELECT is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    61→      .get(listingId) as { is_full: number } | undefined;\n    62→\n    63→    if (currentListing?.is_full) {\n    64→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", status: 400 };\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    // Check if already applied (pending)\n    68→    const existingApp = db\n    69→      .prepare(\n    70→        \"SELECT id, status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    71→      )\n    72→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { id: number; status: string } | undefined;\n    73→\n    74→    if (existingApp) {\n    75→      if (existingApp.status === \"pending\") {\n    76→        return { error: \"You have already applied\", status: 400 };\n    77→      }\n    78→      if (existingApp.status === \"approved\") {\n    79→        return { error: \"You are already approved\", status: 400 };\n    80→      }\n    81→      // If rejected, allow re-application by updating status back to pending\n    82→      db.prepare(\n    83→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'pending', decided_at = NULL, applied_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    84→      ).run(existingApp.id);\n    85→\n    86→      return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    // Insert new application\n    90→    db.prepare(\n    91→      \"INSERT INTO listing_applications (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    92→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    93→\n    94→    return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    95→  });\n    96→\n    97→  const result = applyTransaction();\n    98→\n    99→  if (result.error) {\n   100→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: result.status });\n   101→  }\n   102→\n   103→  // Notify outside transaction (fire-and-forget)\n   104→  notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   105→\n   106→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   107→}\n   108→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"d832fb73-531e-4c70-87a6-09e4d9b8f378","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:01:18.399Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a624d29c4716e91ef","tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","description":"Reading app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":16684,"tool_uses":8,"duration_ms":10781},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"86d51807-8469-45c5-9119-b9688d64693c","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01RviEe1NNkP2NowpJChyxHQ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01DKPfB6Z4ZYayuTTp4gGfL1","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4878,"cache_read_input_tokens":11541,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4878,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"733ef26e-0ebf-4de2-bcab-9ca95e3e867e"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DKPfB6Z4ZYayuTTp4gGfL1","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function PATCH(\n     7→  req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const body = await req.json();\n    34→  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n    35→\n    36→  if (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n    44→  const application = db\n    45→    .prepare(\n    46→      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    47→    )\n    48→    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    49→\n    50→  if (!application) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n    53→      { status: 404 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n    58→\n    59→  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    60→    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    61→    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    62→      // Re-read max_group_size inside transaction to avoid stale data from concurrent PATCH\n    63→      const freshListing = db\n    64→        .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    65→        .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\n    66→\n    67→      if (!freshListing) {\n    68→        throw new Error(\"Listing not found\");\n    69→      }\n    70→\n    71→      // Check if group is already full\n    72→      const { count } = db\n    73→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    74→        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    75→\n    76→      if (count >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    77→        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n    78→      }\n    79→\n    80→      // Update application status\n    81→      db.prepare(\n    82→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    83→      ).run(applicationId);\n    84→\n    85→      // Add to members\n    86→      db.prepare(\n    87→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    88→      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n    89→\n    90→      // Check if group is now full\n    91→      const newCount = count + 1;\n    92→      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n    93→      if (newCount >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    94→        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    95→\n    96→        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n    97→        rejectedApps = db\n    98→          .prepare(\n    99→            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n   100→          )\n   101→          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n   102→\n   103→        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n   104→          db.prepare(\n   105→            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   106→          ).run(app.id);\n   107→        }\n   108→\n   109→        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n   110→      }\n   111→      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n   112→    });\n   113→\n   114→    try {\n   115→      const result = approveTransaction();\n   116→\n   117→      // Send notifications outside the transaction (non-critical)\n   118→      try {\n   119→        notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n   120→\n   121→        if (result.isFull) {\n   122→          notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n   123→          for (const app of result.rejectedApps) {\n   124→            notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   125→          }\n   126→        }\n   127→      } catch (notifErr) {\n   128→        console.error(\"Notification error (approval succeeded):\", notifErr);\n   129→      }\n   130→\n   131→      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   132→    } catch (err) {\n   133→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n   134→      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n   135→    }\n   136→  } else {\n   137→    // Reject\n   138→    db.prepare(\n   139→      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   140→    ).run(applicationId);\n   141→\n   142→    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   143→\n   144→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   145→  }\n   146→}\n   147→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"cbfd167c-08bf-4832-bd12-3cf7fe83e297","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:01:18.447Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01HF4ZaqopLvFQcuU5J8i9Fe","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01GenJ4GLoijRMFVhN4ihUxP","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5058,"cache_read_input_tokens":16419,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5058,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"5db90235-5f09-463a-80fe-6385fb6553b5"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GenJ4GLoijRMFVhN4ihUxP","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→\n     4→const PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n     5→\n     6→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n     7→  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n     8→\n     9→  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n    10→  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n    11→  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n    12→  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n    13→  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n    14→  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n    15→  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n    16→  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n    17→\n    18→  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n    19→  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n    20→\n    21→  // Apply date floor: use the more restrictive of today or startDateFrom\n    22→  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    23→  const hasStartDateFilter = startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom);\n    24→\n    25→  if (!includePast) {\n    26→    // Use whichever is later: today or the explicit start_date_from filter\n    27→    const effectiveDate = hasStartDateFilter && startDateFrom > today ? startDateFrom : today;\n    28→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    29→    params.push(effectiveDate);\n    30→  } else if (hasStartDateFilter) {\n    31→    // includePast is true, but user wants a specific start date floor\n    32→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    33→    params.push(startDateFrom);\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    37→    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    38→    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    42→    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    43→    params.push(meetingFormat);\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    47→    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    48→    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n    49→  }\n    50→\n    51→  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n    52→\n    53→  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n    54→  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n    55→  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n    56→\n    57→  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n    58→\n    59→  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n    60→  const countResult = db\n    61→    .prepare(\n    62→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    63→    )\n    64→    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n    65→\n    66→  const listings = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      `SELECT\n    69→        l.*,\n    70→        u.display_name as author_name,\n    71→        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    72→      FROM listings l\n    73→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    74→      WHERE ${whereClause}\n    75→      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n    76→      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    77→    )\n    78→    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n    79→\n    80→  return NextResponse.json({\n    81→    listings,\n    82→    pagination: {\n    83→      page,\n    84→      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n    85→      total: countResult.total,\n    86→      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    87→    },\n    88→  });\n    89→}\n    90→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"5ee0823a-c40a-44c1-ae69-932733dfdb31","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:01:21.574Z"}
