{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01EySD8i5xKoQs3aKjboULZj","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Here is the full review.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review — Bookmate Next.js Application\n\nReviewing all API routes under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/`, the core libraries under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/`, and the React components under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/` and `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/`.\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL Issues\n\n### 1. `BOT_TOKEN` / `CLIENT_ID` captured at module load time — `isBotConfigured()` is broken\n\n**Confidence: 100**\n\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/telegram.ts`, lines 16–21\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/discord.ts`, lines 16–22\n\n`BOT_TOKEN`, `CLIENT_ID`, and `TELEGRAM_API` / `DISCORD_API` are assigned once at the top of the module when the file is first `import`-ed. In Next.js, module-level code runs when the server bundle is loaded. If these env vars are absent at that point (e.g., `.env.local` isn't loaded yet in certain test/build contexts, or the vars are injected at a later stage), the constants are permanently `\"\"`.\n\nMore critically, `isBotConfigured()` checks `BOT_TOKEN.length > 0`, but `BOT_TOKEN` is the *snapshot* taken at load time, not a live read of `process.env`. The actual API fetch URL `TELEGRAM_API` is also frozen at load time, so calling `callApi(...)` in `telegram.ts` will silently POST to `https://api.telegram.org/bot/method` (an invalid URL) rather than the real bot URL.\n\n`notifications.ts` line 133–134 correctly reads `process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` live, creating an inconsistency: notifications may say \"bot will set up group automatically\" while `isBotConfigured()` returns `false`.\n\n**Fix:** Read the env vars inside each function call, not at module scope:\n\n```ts\n// telegram.ts\nexport function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n  return !!(process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN);\n}\n\nfunction getTelegramApi(): string {\n  return `https://api.telegram.org/bot${process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}`;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on foreign key constraints — orphaned rows guaranteed on deletion\n\n**Confidence: 100**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`, lines 45, 53–54, 59–62, 73–74, 86–88, 131–133, 148–149, 162–165\n\nEvery `FOREIGN KEY` in the schema lacks `ON DELETE CASCADE`. The `DELETE` handler for listings (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 355–362) manually deletes dependent rows before deleting the parent listing, which partially mitigates this. However, there is **no equivalent cleanup path for user deletion** — there is no user-delete endpoint, but if one is ever added, `listing_members`, `notifications`, `user_genres`, `ratings`, `telegram_user_links`, and `listing_applications` rows will be orphaned.\n\nMore acutely: the `pending_telegram_groups` table (line 131–133) uses `FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)` without cascade. If a listing is deleted and the manual `DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups` in the transaction fails or is skipped, the orphaned row will cause the Telegram webhook to attempt to link a non-existent listing (line 127 of the webhook route). The transaction does cover this case, but only by manual ordering — a future developer adding another child table will miss it.\n\n**Fix:** Add `ON DELETE CASCADE` to all FK constraints. Because SQLite doesn't support `ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT`, this requires a schema migration (recreate tables). At minimum the most risk-bearing ones (`listing_members`, `listing_applications`, `pending_telegram_groups`) should be migrated first.\n\n---\n\n### 3. `requireAuth` does not verify the user still exists in the database\n\n**Confidence: 100**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, lines 37–43\n\n`requireAuth()` only checks that `session.userId` is truthy in the iron-session cookie. It does **not** query the database to confirm the user still exists. This means:\n\n- A deleted user (if deletion is ever added) retains a valid session and can create listings, join groups, and submit ratings.\n- More concretely right now: `session.displayName` is the value stored in the cookie at login time. If the user updates their display name via `PATCH /api/profile`, the cookie is updated (`session.save()` is called), but this update relies on the profile update route correctly calling `session.save()`. If that ever fails silently, notifications will be sent with stale names.\n- Any endpoint that reads `session.displayName` for notification text (join/route.ts line 113, apply/route.ts line 104) trusts the session value, not the DB value.\n\nThe `/api/auth/me` route does perform a DB check and destroys stale sessions (lines 18–21), but this only runs when that specific endpoint is polled. All other `requireAuth()` callers do not benefit from it.\n\n**Fix:** Add a DB existence check inside `requireAuth()`:\n\n```ts\nexport async function requireAuth() {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) return null;\n  const user = db.prepare(\"SELECT id, display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?\").get(session.userId);\n  if (!user) {\n    await session.destroy();\n    return null;\n  }\n  return session;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## IMPORTANT Issues\n\n### 4. `notifyGroupFull` reads `platform_preference` outside the join transaction — stale data risk\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 121–148\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 110–114\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, lines 121–122\n\n`notifyGroupFull()` performs a fresh `SELECT book_title, platform_preference, author_id` query at call time, which is *after* the join transaction commits. Between the transaction commit and this query, a concurrent `PATCH /api/listings/[id]` could change `platform_preference`. The notification message would then say \"Discord group will be set up automatically\" when the actual preference is Telegram (or vice versa), confusing all group members.\n\nThis is a known pre-existing concern, but it is still unresolved. The fix is to pass the relevant fields as parameters to `notifyGroupFull` rather than re-querying:\n\n```ts\nexport function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number, bookTitle: string, platformPreference: string) {\n  // use passed-in values instead of re-querying\n}\n```\n\nThe callers already have the listing data available from their own pre-transaction reads (or can capture it inside the transaction).\n\n---\n\n### 5. `PATCH /api/listings/[id]` — listing data read outside the transaction, creating a TOCTOU on `telegram_link`/`discord_link` guard\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 133–151\n\nThe guard that prevents editing a listing after a platform link has been shared (lines 146–151) reads `listing` from a query at line 133–135, *before* the update transaction at lines 261–299. A concurrent `POST /api/listings/[id]/telegram` or `/discord` could set `telegram_link` between this check and the transaction, causing the `PATCH` to overwrite a listing that already has a live group chat link (e.g., changing `max_group_size` or `platform_preference` after a Telegram group is already set up).\n\n**Fix:** Re-read `telegram_link` and `discord_link` inside the `updateTransaction` and abort if either is non-empty:\n\n```ts\nconst updateTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n  const current = db.prepare(\"SELECT telegram_link, discord_link FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as ...;\n  if (current?.telegram_link || current?.discord_link) {\n    return { error: \"Cannot edit after group chat link has been shared\" };\n  }\n  // ... rest of update\n});\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. `session.displayName` used for notification text — reflects stale cookie value, not actual DB name\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, line 113\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`, line 104\n\nWhen a user joins or applies to a group, their display name in the notification to the author comes from `session.displayName`, which is the value baked into the iron-session cookie. If the user changes their display name via `PATCH /api/profile`, the cookie is updated in that request. But because iron-session cookies are signed+encrypted client-side values, any tab that was open before the profile update will still carry the old cookie until the browser reloads.\n\nMore critically, the session cookie has a 7-day lifetime. A user who updated their name 3 days ago but still has an old cookie open in another tab will send notifications with their old name. Group organizers will see the wrong name in the notification.\n\n**Fix:** Fetch the current `display_name` directly from the DB in these two routes rather than trusting the session value:\n\n```ts\nconst user = db.prepare(\"SELECT display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?\").get(session.userId) as { display_name: string } | undefined;\nconst displayName = user?.display_name || \"Someone\";\nnotifyListingAuthor(listingId, displayName);\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7. `PATCH /api/profile` — no type validation on `displayName`/`bio` before string operations\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/route.ts`, lines 11–28\n\n`displayName` and `bio` are destructured from `req.json()` with no type check before `.trim()` and `.length` are accessed. If a client sends `{ \"displayName\": 42 }`, `displayName.trim()` will throw a `TypeError` (numbers don't have `.trim()`), resulting in an unhandled exception that Next.js will catch and return a generic 500. This exposes an unintended error path, and similar code in the register route correctly defends against this with `typeof` checks.\n\n**Fix:** Add type guards:\n\n```ts\nif (typeof displayName !== \"string\") {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Display name must be a string\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\nif (bio !== undefined && typeof bio !== \"string\") {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Bio must be a string\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 8. `email.ts` — `isConfigured` evaluated at module load time, SMTP config changes not picked up\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/email.ts`, lines 3–6, 10–12\n\n`isConfigured` is a module-level constant computed from `process.env` values at import time (identical pattern to the BOT_TOKEN issue). If SMTP env vars are set after module initialization (e.g., via a process manager reloading env, or in tests that set env vars after imports), `isConfigured` will be `false` permanently and no emails will be sent — failing silently.\n\nAdditionally, the `transporter` singleton is created lazily but is never recreated if SMTP credentials change between calls. This is lower risk than `isConfigured` but worth noting.\n\n**Fix:** Compute `isConfigured` inside `getTransporter()`:\n\n```ts\nfunction getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n  if (!process.env.SMTP_HOST || !process.env.SMTP_PORT || !process.env.SMTP_FROM) return null;\n  // ... create/return transporter\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 9. Discord webhook `handleLink` — `guildId` validated as snowflake but passed directly into Discord API URL path without additional sanitization in `createTextChannel`\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts`, lines 80–85, 125–130\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/discord.ts`, lines 101–111\n\n`guildId` is validated with `isValidSnowflake()` (17–20 digits) before being passed to `createTextChannel(guildId, ...)`. Inside `createTextChannel`, `guildId` is interpolated directly into the fetch URL: `` `/guilds/${guildId}/channels` ``. The snowflake validation is correct and sufficient for the Discord API. However, `channelId` (when provided directly in the webhook body) is also validated with `isValidSnowflake`, then passed to `createChannelInvite(targetChannelId)`, which interpolates it into `` `/channels/${channelId}/invites` ``. This is fine.\n\nThe real risk is that the webhook caller (protected only by `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET`) can supply any `listingId`, and there's no check that the provided `guildId` actually belongs to the bot or to a guild where the listing author is a member. A secret-holder could link any listing to any Discord server. However, since `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET` is a shared secret intended for bot-to-server communication, this is an architectural decision rather than a pure bug. Flagging at IMPORTANT because it means any party with the webhook secret can set or hijack Discord links for any listing.\n\n**Fix:** After fetching the listing, verify that the guild is one the bot is actually a member of by calling `getGuild(guildId)` and confirming it returns successfully before proceeding.\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | File | Lines | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|-------|-------|\n| 1 | CRITICAL | `lib/telegram.ts`, `lib/discord.ts` | 16–21, 16–22 | BOT_TOKEN/CLIENT_ID frozen at module load; `isBotConfigured()` and API calls will silently use stale/empty values |\n| 2 | CRITICAL | `lib/db.ts` | 45, 53–88, 131–165 | No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on any FK; orphaned data inevitable without exhaustive manual cleanup |\n| 3 | CRITICAL | `lib/session.ts` | 37–43 | `requireAuth` never re-validates user existence in DB; deleted users retain full session access |\n| 4 | IMPORTANT | `lib/notifications.ts` | 121–148 | `notifyGroupFull` re-queries `platform_preference` outside transaction; can produce stale notification message |\n| 5 | IMPORTANT | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` | 133–151, 261–299 | `telegram_link`/`discord_link` guard read outside the update transaction; TOCTOU race allows editing after group chat is set up |\n| 6 | IMPORTANT | `app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, `apply/route.ts` | 113, 104 | `session.displayName` used for notifications — reflects cookie value, not current DB name |\n| 7 | IMPORTANT | `app/api/profile/route.ts` | 11–28 | No type validation on `displayName`/`bio`; non-string input causes unhandled `TypeError` → 500 |\n| 8 | IMPORTANT | `lib/email.ts` | 3–6 | `isConfigured` frozen at module load time; SMTP config changes not honoured without server restart |\n| 9 | IMPORTANT | `app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts` | 58–113 | Webhook secret holder can link any listing to any Discord guild; no guild membership verification |"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a624d29c4716e91ef (use SendMessage with to: 'a624d29c4716e91ef' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 84166\ntool_uses: 44\nduration_ms: 140142</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"b8d730cb-6c10-445f-ada5-c330dd67ddd6","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:03:27.804Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Do a thorough code review of the Bookmate Next.js application at /root/Projects/bookmate/app. This is a reading group matchmaking web app using Next.js 15, SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session, and Tailwind CSS.\n\nFocus areas (these were noted but not fixed in previous reviews):\n1. `notifyGroupFull` reads `platform_preference` outside the join transaction — potential stale data\n2. `BOT_TOKEN` / `CLIENT_ID` captured at module load time — won't pick up env changes\n3. No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on FK constraints — orphaned data on user/listing deletion\n4. `requireAuth` does not re-validate user existence in DB\n\nAlso look for NEW issues in these files:\n- app/api/ — all API routes\n- app/lib/ — core libs (db, session, notifications)\n- app/components/ — React components\n- app/app/ — pages\n\nFocus on:\n- Race conditions and concurrency bugs\n- Security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, XSS, auth bypass)\n- Data integrity issues\n- Error handling gaps\n- Logic errors\n- TypeScript type safety issues\n\nDo NOT report:\n- Style/formatting issues\n- Missing features\n- Performance optimizations (unless critical)\n- Issues already fixed in recent commits\n\nFor each bug found, report: severity (CRITICAL/IMPORTANT/MINOR), file path, line numbers, what's wrong, and how to fix it.","agentId":"a624d29c4716e91ef","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Here is the full review.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review — Bookmate Next.js Application\n\nReviewing all API routes under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/`, the core libraries under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/`, and the React components under `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/` and `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/`.\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL Issues\n\n### 1. `BOT_TOKEN` / `CLIENT_ID` captured at module load time — `isBotConfigured()` is broken\n\n**Confidence: 100**\n\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/telegram.ts`, lines 16–21\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/discord.ts`, lines 16–22\n\n`BOT_TOKEN`, `CLIENT_ID`, and `TELEGRAM_API` / `DISCORD_API` are assigned once at the top of the module when the file is first `import`-ed. In Next.js, module-level code runs when the server bundle is loaded. If these env vars are absent at that point (e.g., `.env.local` isn't loaded yet in certain test/build contexts, or the vars are injected at a later stage), the constants are permanently `\"\"`.\n\nMore critically, `isBotConfigured()` checks `BOT_TOKEN.length > 0`, but `BOT_TOKEN` is the *snapshot* taken at load time, not a live read of `process.env`. The actual API fetch URL `TELEGRAM_API` is also frozen at load time, so calling `callApi(...)` in `telegram.ts` will silently POST to `https://api.telegram.org/bot/method` (an invalid URL) rather than the real bot URL.\n\n`notifications.ts` line 133–134 correctly reads `process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` live, creating an inconsistency: notifications may say \"bot will set up group automatically\" while `isBotConfigured()` returns `false`.\n\n**Fix:** Read the env vars inside each function call, not at module scope:\n\n```ts\n// telegram.ts\nexport function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n  return !!(process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN);\n}\n\nfunction getTelegramApi(): string {\n  return `https://api.telegram.org/bot${process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}`;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on foreign key constraints — orphaned rows guaranteed on deletion\n\n**Confidence: 100**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/db.ts`, lines 45, 53–54, 59–62, 73–74, 86–88, 131–133, 148–149, 162–165\n\nEvery `FOREIGN KEY` in the schema lacks `ON DELETE CASCADE`. The `DELETE` handler for listings (`/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 355–362) manually deletes dependent rows before deleting the parent listing, which partially mitigates this. However, there is **no equivalent cleanup path for user deletion** — there is no user-delete endpoint, but if one is ever added, `listing_members`, `notifications`, `user_genres`, `ratings`, `telegram_user_links`, and `listing_applications` rows will be orphaned.\n\nMore acutely: the `pending_telegram_groups` table (line 131–133) uses `FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)` without cascade. If a listing is deleted and the manual `DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups` in the transaction fails or is skipped, the orphaned row will cause the Telegram webhook to attempt to link a non-existent listing (line 127 of the webhook route). The transaction does cover this case, but only by manual ordering — a future developer adding another child table will miss it.\n\n**Fix:** Add `ON DELETE CASCADE` to all FK constraints. Because SQLite doesn't support `ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT`, this requires a schema migration (recreate tables). At minimum the most risk-bearing ones (`listing_members`, `listing_applications`, `pending_telegram_groups`) should be migrated first.\n\n---\n\n### 3. `requireAuth` does not verify the user still exists in the database\n\n**Confidence: 100**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/session.ts`, lines 37–43\n\n`requireAuth()` only checks that `session.userId` is truthy in the iron-session cookie. It does **not** query the database to confirm the user still exists. This means:\n\n- A deleted user (if deletion is ever added) retains a valid session and can create listings, join groups, and submit ratings.\n- More concretely right now: `session.displayName` is the value stored in the cookie at login time. If the user updates their display name via `PATCH /api/profile`, the cookie is updated (`session.save()` is called), but this update relies on the profile update route correctly calling `session.save()`. If that ever fails silently, notifications will be sent with stale names.\n- Any endpoint that reads `session.displayName` for notification text (join/route.ts line 113, apply/route.ts line 104) trusts the session value, not the DB value.\n\nThe `/api/auth/me` route does perform a DB check and destroys stale sessions (lines 18–21), but this only runs when that specific endpoint is polled. All other `requireAuth()` callers do not benefit from it.\n\n**Fix:** Add a DB existence check inside `requireAuth()`:\n\n```ts\nexport async function requireAuth() {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) return null;\n  const user = db.prepare(\"SELECT id, display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?\").get(session.userId);\n  if (!user) {\n    await session.destroy();\n    return null;\n  }\n  return session;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## IMPORTANT Issues\n\n### 4. `notifyGroupFull` reads `platform_preference` outside the join transaction — stale data risk\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 121–148\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 110–114\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, lines 121–122\n\n`notifyGroupFull()` performs a fresh `SELECT book_title, platform_preference, author_id` query at call time, which is *after* the join transaction commits. Between the transaction commit and this query, a concurrent `PATCH /api/listings/[id]` could change `platform_preference`. The notification message would then say \"Discord group will be set up automatically\" when the actual preference is Telegram (or vice versa), confusing all group members.\n\nThis is a known pre-existing concern, but it is still unresolved. The fix is to pass the relevant fields as parameters to `notifyGroupFull` rather than re-querying:\n\n```ts\nexport function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number, bookTitle: string, platformPreference: string) {\n  // use passed-in values instead of re-querying\n}\n```\n\nThe callers already have the listing data available from their own pre-transaction reads (or can capture it inside the transaction).\n\n---\n\n### 5. `PATCH /api/listings/[id]` — listing data read outside the transaction, creating a TOCTOU on `telegram_link`/`discord_link` guard\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 133–151\n\nThe guard that prevents editing a listing after a platform link has been shared (lines 146–151) reads `listing` from a query at line 133–135, *before* the update transaction at lines 261–299. A concurrent `POST /api/listings/[id]/telegram` or `/discord` could set `telegram_link` between this check and the transaction, causing the `PATCH` to overwrite a listing that already has a live group chat link (e.g., changing `max_group_size` or `platform_preference` after a Telegram group is already set up).\n\n**Fix:** Re-read `telegram_link` and `discord_link` inside the `updateTransaction` and abort if either is non-empty:\n\n```ts\nconst updateTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n  const current = db.prepare(\"SELECT telegram_link, discord_link FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as ...;\n  if (current?.telegram_link || current?.discord_link) {\n    return { error: \"Cannot edit after group chat link has been shared\" };\n  }\n  // ... rest of update\n});\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. `session.displayName` used for notification text — reflects stale cookie value, not actual DB name\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, line 113\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`, line 104\n\nWhen a user joins or applies to a group, their display name in the notification to the author comes from `session.displayName`, which is the value baked into the iron-session cookie. If the user changes their display name via `PATCH /api/profile`, the cookie is updated in that request. But because iron-session cookies are signed+encrypted client-side values, any tab that was open before the profile update will still carry the old cookie until the browser reloads.\n\nMore critically, the session cookie has a 7-day lifetime. A user who updated their name 3 days ago but still has an old cookie open in another tab will send notifications with their old name. Group organizers will see the wrong name in the notification.\n\n**Fix:** Fetch the current `display_name` directly from the DB in these two routes rather than trusting the session value:\n\n```ts\nconst user = db.prepare(\"SELECT display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?\").get(session.userId) as { display_name: string } | undefined;\nconst displayName = user?.display_name || \"Someone\";\nnotifyListingAuthor(listingId, displayName);\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7. `PATCH /api/profile` — no type validation on `displayName`/`bio` before string operations\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/route.ts`, lines 11–28\n\n`displayName` and `bio` are destructured from `req.json()` with no type check before `.trim()` and `.length` are accessed. If a client sends `{ \"displayName\": 42 }`, `displayName.trim()` will throw a `TypeError` (numbers don't have `.trim()`), resulting in an unhandled exception that Next.js will catch and return a generic 500. This exposes an unintended error path, and similar code in the register route correctly defends against this with `typeof` checks.\n\n**Fix:** Add type guards:\n\n```ts\nif (typeof displayName !== \"string\") {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Display name must be a string\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\nif (bio !== undefined && typeof bio !== \"string\") {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Bio must be a string\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 8. `email.ts` — `isConfigured` evaluated at module load time, SMTP config changes not picked up\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/email.ts`, lines 3–6, 10–12\n\n`isConfigured` is a module-level constant computed from `process.env` values at import time (identical pattern to the BOT_TOKEN issue). If SMTP env vars are set after module initialization (e.g., via a process manager reloading env, or in tests that set env vars after imports), `isConfigured` will be `false` permanently and no emails will be sent — failing silently.\n\nAdditionally, the `transporter` singleton is created lazily but is never recreated if SMTP credentials change between calls. This is lower risk than `isConfigured` but worth noting.\n\n**Fix:** Compute `isConfigured` inside `getTransporter()`:\n\n```ts\nfunction getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n  if (!process.env.SMTP_HOST || !process.env.SMTP_PORT || !process.env.SMTP_FROM) return null;\n  // ... create/return transporter\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 9. Discord webhook `handleLink` — `guildId` validated as snowflake but passed directly into Discord API URL path without additional sanitization in `createTextChannel`\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts`, lines 80–85, 125–130\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/discord.ts`, lines 101–111\n\n`guildId` is validated with `isValidSnowflake()` (17–20 digits) before being passed to `createTextChannel(guildId, ...)`. Inside `createTextChannel`, `guildId` is interpolated directly into the fetch URL: `` `/guilds/${guildId}/channels` ``. The snowflake validation is correct and sufficient for the Discord API. However, `channelId` (when provided directly in the webhook body) is also validated with `isValidSnowflake`, then passed to `createChannelInvite(targetChannelId)`, which interpolates it into `` `/channels/${channelId}/invites` ``. This is fine.\n\nThe real risk is that the webhook caller (protected only by `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET`) can supply any `listingId`, and there's no check that the provided `guildId` actually belongs to the bot or to a guild where the listing author is a member. A secret-holder could link any listing to any Discord server. However, since `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET` is a shared secret intended for bot-to-server communication, this is an architectural decision rather than a pure bug. Flagging at IMPORTANT because it means any party with the webhook secret can set or hijack Discord links for any listing.\n\n**Fix:** After fetching the listing, verify that the guild is one the bot is actually a member of by calling `getGuild(guildId)` and confirming it returns successfully before proceeding.\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | File | Lines | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|-------|-------|\n| 1 | CRITICAL | `lib/telegram.ts`, `lib/discord.ts` | 16–21, 16–22 | BOT_TOKEN/CLIENT_ID frozen at module load; `isBotConfigured()` and API calls will silently use stale/empty values |\n| 2 | CRITICAL | `lib/db.ts` | 45, 53–88, 131–165 | No `ON DELETE CASCADE` on any FK; orphaned data inevitable without exhaustive manual cleanup |\n| 3 | CRITICAL | `lib/session.ts` | 37–43 | `requireAuth` never re-validates user existence in DB; deleted users retain full session access |\n| 4 | IMPORTANT | `lib/notifications.ts` | 121–148 | `notifyGroupFull` re-queries `platform_preference` outside transaction; can produce stale notification message |\n| 5 | IMPORTANT | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` | 133–151, 261–299 | `telegram_link`/`discord_link` guard read outside the update transaction; TOCTOU race allows editing after group chat is set up |\n| 6 | IMPORTANT | `app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, `apply/route.ts` | 113, 104 | `session.displayName` used for notifications — reflects cookie value, not current DB name |\n| 7 | IMPORTANT | `app/api/profile/route.ts` | 11–28 | No type validation on `displayName`/`bio`; non-string input causes unhandled `TypeError` → 500 |\n| 8 | IMPORTANT | `lib/email.ts` | 3–6 | `isConfigured` frozen at module load time; SMTP config changes not honoured without server restart |\n| 9 | IMPORTANT | `app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts` | 58–113 | Webhook secret holder can link any listing to any Discord guild; no guild membership verification |"}],"totalDurationMs":140142,"totalTokens":84166,"totalToolUseCount":44,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":468,"cache_read_input_tokens":79693,"output_tokens":4004,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":468},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GGmg2zxmv3ujpdBKYSq3nd","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Telegram Bot API integration for automatic group creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate deep links for one-click group creation\n     6→ * 2. Detect when the bot is added to a group (via webhook)\n     7→ * 3. Auto-generate and save invite links\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Create Telegram Group\" → opens deep link in Telegram\n    11→ * - Telegram prompts author to create a group with the bot\n    12→ * - Bot receives `my_chat_member` update via webhook\n    13→ * - Bot creates an invite link and saves it to the listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const BOT_TOKEN = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    17→const TELEGRAM_API = `https://api.telegram.org/bot${BOT_TOKEN}`;\n    18→\n    19→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    20→  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0;\n    21→}\n    22→\n    23→/**\n    24→ * Call the Telegram Bot API.\n    25→ */\n    26→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    27→  method: string,\n    28→  params?: Record<string, unknown>\n    29→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; result?: T; description?: string }> {\n    30→  const res = await fetch(`${TELEGRAM_API}/${method}`, {\n    31→    method: \"POST\",\n    32→    headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    33→    body: JSON.stringify(params || {}),\n    34→  });\n    35→  return res.json();\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→/**\n    39→ * Get bot info (username, id).\n    40→ */\n    41→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    42→  id: number;\n    43→  username: string;\n    44→  first_name: string;\n    45→} | null> {\n    46→  const res = await callApi<{\n    47→    id: number;\n    48→    username: string;\n    49→    first_name: string;\n    50→  }>(\"getMe\");\n    51→  return res.ok ? res.result! : null;\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→/**\n    55→ * Generate a deep link that opens Telegram and prompts the user\n    56→ * to create a new group with the bot already added.\n    57→ *\n    58→ * Format: https://t.me/BOT_USERNAME?startgroup=listing_LISTING_ID\n    59→ *\n    60→ * When the user creates the group, Telegram adds the bot and\n    61→ * sends a `my_chat_member` update to our webhook.\n    62→ */\n    63→export async function generateGroupDeepLink(\n    64→  listingId: number\n    65→): Promise<string | null> {\n    66→  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n    67→  if (!bot) return null;\n    68→  return `https://t.me/${bot.username}?startgroup=listing_${listingId}`;\n    69→}\n    70→\n    71→/**\n    72→ * Create an invite link for a chat where the bot is an admin.\n    73→ */\n    74→export async function createChatInviteLink(\n    75→  chatId: number | string\n    76→): Promise<string | null> {\n    77→  const res = await callApi<{ invite_link: string }>(\n    78→    \"createChatInviteLink\",\n    79→    {\n    80→      chat_id: chatId,\n    81→      name: \"Bookmate Reading Group\",\n    82→      creates_join_request: false,\n    83→    }\n    84→  );\n    85→  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n    86→    return res.result.invite_link;\n    87→  }\n    88→  return null;\n    89→}\n    90→\n    91→/**\n    92→ * Export the existing invite link for a chat (fallback).\n    93→ */\n    94→export async function exportChatInviteLink(\n    95→  chatId: number | string\n    96→): Promise<string | null> {\n    97→  const res = await callApi<string>(\"exportChatInviteLink\", {\n    98→    chat_id: chatId,\n    99→  });\n   100→  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n   101→    return res.result;\n   102→  }\n   103→  return null;\n   104→}\n   105→\n   106→/**\n   107→ * Escape HTML special characters for Telegram HTML parse mode.\n   108→ * Telegram's HTML parser interprets <, >, &, and \" — all must be escaped\n   109→ * in user-supplied content to prevent message injection.\n   110→ */\n   111→export function escapeHtml(text: string): string {\n   112→  return text\n   113→    .replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\")\n   114→    .replace(/</g, \"&lt;\")\n   115→    .replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\")\n   116→    .replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→/**\n   120→ * Send a message to a Telegram chat.\n   121→ */\n   122→export async function sendMessage(\n   123→  chatId: number | string,\n   124→  text: string\n   125→): Promise<boolean> {\n   126→  const res = await callApi(\"sendMessage\", {\n   127→    chat_id: chatId,\n   128→    text,\n   129→    parse_mode: \"HTML\",\n   130→  });\n   131→  return res.ok;\n   132→}\n   133→\n   134→/**\n   135→ * Set up webhook URL for receiving Telegram updates.\n   136→ */\n   137→export async function setWebhook(url: string): Promise<boolean> {\n   138→  const res = await callApi(\"setWebhook\", {\n   139→    url,\n   140→    allowed_updates: [\"my_chat_member\", \"message\"],\n   141→  });\n   142→  return res.ok;\n   143→}\n   144→\n   145→/**\n   146→ * Parse the listing ID from a /start command payload.\n   147→ * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n   148→ */\n   149→export function parseListingIdFromPayload(\n   150→  payload: string\n   151→): number | null {\n   152→  const match = payload.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n   153→  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n   154→}\n   155→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"901b6ba4-0f1b-4f29-b3e6-29c4985549c1","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:03:40.579Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/telegram.ts","content":"/**\n * Telegram Bot API integration for automatic group creation.\n *\n * When TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is set, the system can:\n * 1. Generate deep links for one-click group creation\n * 2. Detect when the bot is added to a group (via webhook)\n * 3. Auto-generate and save invite links\n *\n * Flow:\n * - Author clicks \"Create Telegram Group\" → opens deep link in Telegram\n * - Telegram prompts author to create a group with the bot\n * - Bot receives `my_chat_member` update via webhook\n * - Bot creates an invite link and saves it to the listing\n */\n\nconst BOT_TOKEN = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\nconst TELEGRAM_API = `https://api.telegram.org/bot${BOT_TOKEN}`;\n\nexport function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * Call the Telegram Bot API.\n */\nasync function callApi<T = unknown>(\n  method: string,\n  params?: Record<string, unknown>\n): Promise<{ ok: boolean; result?: T; description?: string }> {\n  const res = await fetch(`${TELEGRAM_API}/${method}`, {\n    method: \"POST\",\n    headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    body: JSON.stringify(params || {}),\n  });\n  return res.json();\n}\n\n/**\n * Get bot info (username, id).\n */\nexport async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n  id: number;\n  username: string;\n  first_name: string;\n} | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{\n    id: number;\n    username: string;\n    first_name: string;\n  }>(\"getMe\");\n  return res.ok ? res.result! : null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate a deep link that opens Telegram and prompts the user\n * to create a new group with the bot already added.\n *\n * Format: https://t.me/BOT_USERNAME?startgroup=listing_LISTING_ID\n *\n * When the user creates the group, Telegram adds the bot and\n * sends a `my_chat_member` update to our webhook.\n */\nexport async function generateGroupDeepLink(\n  listingId: number\n): Promise<string | null> {\n  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n  if (!bot) return null;\n  return `https://t.me/${bot.username}?startgroup=listing_${listingId}`;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create an invite link for a chat where the bot is an admin.\n */\nexport async function createChatInviteLink(\n  chatId: number | string\n): Promise<string | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ invite_link: string }>(\n    \"createChatInviteLink\",\n    {\n      chat_id: chatId,\n      name: \"Bookmate Reading Group\",\n      creates_join_request: false,\n    }\n  );\n  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n    return res.result.invite_link;\n  }\n  return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Export the existing invite link for a chat (fallback).\n */\nexport async function exportChatInviteLink(\n  chatId: number | string\n): Promise<string | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<string>(\"exportChatInviteLink\", {\n    chat_id: chatId,\n  });\n  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n    return res.result;\n  }\n  return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Escape HTML special characters for Telegram HTML parse mode.\n * Telegram's HTML parser interprets <, >, &, and \" — all must be escaped\n * in user-supplied content to prevent message injection.\n */\nexport function escapeHtml(text: string): string {\n  return text\n    .replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\")\n    .replace(/</g, \"&lt;\")\n    .replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\")\n    .replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Send a message to a Telegram chat.\n */\nexport async function sendMessage(\n  chatId: number | string,\n  text: string\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  const res = await callApi(\"sendMessage\", {\n    chat_id: chatId,\n    text,\n    parse_mode: \"HTML\",\n  });\n  return res.ok;\n}\n\n/**\n * Set up webhook URL for receiving Telegram updates.\n */\nexport async function setWebhook(url: string): Promise<boolean> {\n  const res = await callApi(\"setWebhook\", {\n    url,\n    allowed_updates: [\"my_chat_member\", \"message\"],\n  });\n  return res.ok;\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse the listing ID from a /start command payload.\n * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n */\nexport function parseListingIdFromPayload(\n  payload: string\n): number | null {\n  const match = payload.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n}\n","numLines":155,"startLine":1,"totalLines":155}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01ATLEd3x1tsjFTJWNwQ3UAG","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Discord Bot API integration for automatic server/channel creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID are set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate bot invite URLs so authors can add the bot to their server\n     6→ * 2. Create text channels and invite links via Discord REST API\n     7→ * 3. Receive interaction events via webhook\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Set up Discord\" → opens bot invite URL\n    11→ * - Author adds bot to their server\n    12→ * - Bot receives an interaction or the author triggers channel creation\n    13→ * - Bot creates a text channel + invite link, saves to listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const BOT_TOKEN = process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    17→const CLIENT_ID = process.env.DISCORD_CLIENT_ID || \"\";\n    18→const DISCORD_API = \"https://discord.com/api/v10\";\n    19→\n    20→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    21→  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0 && CLIENT_ID.length > 0;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→/**\n    25→ * Validate a Discord snowflake ID (numeric string, 17-20 digits).\n    26→ * Used to prevent path traversal in Discord API URL construction.\n    27→ */\n    28→export function isValidSnowflake(id: string): boolean {\n    29→  return /^\\d{17,20}$/.test(id);\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→/**\n    33→ * Call the Discord REST API.\n    34→ */\n    35→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    36→  endpoint: string,\n    37→  method: string = \"GET\",\n    38→  body?: Record<string, unknown>\n    39→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; data?: T; error?: string }> {\n    40→  try {\n    41→    const res = await fetch(`${DISCORD_API}${endpoint}`, {\n    42→      method,\n    43→      headers: {\n    44→        Authorization: `Bot ${BOT_TOKEN}`,\n    45→        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n    46→      },\n    47→      body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,\n    48→    });\n    49→\n    50→    if (!res.ok) {\n    51→      const errData = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));\n    52→      return {\n    53→        ok: false,\n    54→        error: (errData as Record<string, string>).message || `HTTP ${res.status}`,\n    55→      };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    const data = (await res.json()) as T;\n    59→    return { ok: true, data };\n    60→  } catch (err) {\n    61→    return { ok: false, error: String(err) };\n    62→  }\n    63→}\n    64→\n    65→/**\n    66→ * Get bot user info.\n    67→ */\n    68→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    69→  id: string;\n    70→  username: string;\n    71→} | null> {\n    72→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; username: string }>(\"/users/@me\");\n    73→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n    74→}\n    75→\n    76→/**\n    77→ * Generate a bot invite URL that includes the listing ID in the state parameter.\n    78→ * The bot needs permissions: Manage Channels, Create Instant Invite, Send Messages\n    79→ *\n    80→ * Permission bits:\n    81→ * - MANAGE_CHANNELS (0x10) = 16\n    82→ * - CREATE_INSTANT_INVITE (0x1) = 1\n    83→ * - SEND_MESSAGES (0x800) = 2048\n    84→ * - VIEW_CHANNEL (0x400) = 1024\n    85→ * Total: 3089\n    86→ */\n    87→export function generateBotInviteUrl(listingId: number): string {\n    88→  const permissions = 3089;\n    89→  const state = `listing_${listingId}`;\n    90→  return `https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=${CLIENT_ID}&permissions=${permissions}&scope=bot&state=${encodeURIComponent(state)}`;\n    91→}\n    92→\n    93→/**\n    94→ * Create a text channel in a guild.\n    95→ */\n    96→export async function createTextChannel(\n    97→  guildId: string,\n    98→  name: string,\n    99→  topic?: string\n   100→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n   101→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n   102→    `/guilds/${guildId}/channels`,\n   103→    \"POST\",\n   104→    {\n   105→      name: name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, \"-\").slice(0, 100),\n   106→      type: 0, // GUILD_TEXT\n   107→      topic: topic || \"Bookmate reading group discussion channel\",\n   108→    }\n   109→  );\n   110→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   111→}\n   112→\n   113→/**\n   114→ * Create an invite for a channel.\n   115→ */\n   116→export async function createChannelInvite(\n   117→  channelId: string,\n   118→  maxAge: number = 0 // 0 = never expires\n   119→): Promise<string | null> {\n   120→  const res = await callApi<{ code: string }>(\n   121→    `/channels/${channelId}/invites`,\n   122→    \"POST\",\n   123→    {\n   124→      max_age: maxAge,\n   125→      max_uses: 0,\n   126→      unique: true,\n   127→    }\n   128→  );\n   129→  if (res.ok && res.data) {\n   130→    return `https://discord.gg/${res.data.code}`;\n   131→  }\n   132→  return null;\n   133→}\n   134→\n   135→/**\n   136→ * Send a message to a Discord channel.\n   137→ */\n   138→export async function sendChannelMessage(\n   139→  channelId: string,\n   140→  content: string\n   141→): Promise<boolean> {\n   142→  const res = await callApi(\n   143→    `/channels/${channelId}/messages`,\n   144→    \"POST\",\n   145→    { content }\n   146→  );\n   147→  return res.ok;\n   148→}\n   149→\n   150→/**\n   151→ * Get guild (server) info.\n   152→ */\n   153→export async function getGuild(\n   154→  guildId: string\n   155→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n   156→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n   157→    `/guilds/${guildId}`\n   158→  );\n   159→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   160→}\n   161→\n   162→/**\n   163→ * List guilds the bot is a member of.\n   164→ */\n   165→export async function getBotGuilds(): Promise<\n   166→  { id: string; name: string }[]\n   167→> {\n   168→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }[]>(\n   169→    \"/users/@me/guilds\"\n   170→  );\n   171→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : [];\n   172→}\n   173→\n   174→/**\n   175→ * Parse listing ID from a state or payload string.\n   176→ * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n   177→ */\n   178→export function parseListingIdFromState(state: string): number | null {\n   179→  const match = state.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n   180→  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n   181→}\n   182→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"3117a0f8-ca73-41de-bbe9-f6d550e1f277","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:03:40.620Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/discord.ts","content":"/**\n * Discord Bot API integration for automatic server/channel creation.\n *\n * When DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID are set, the system can:\n * 1. Generate bot invite URLs so authors can add the bot to their server\n * 2. Create text channels and invite links via Discord REST API\n * 3. Receive interaction events via webhook\n *\n * Flow:\n * - Author clicks \"Set up Discord\" → opens bot invite URL\n * - Author adds bot to their server\n * - Bot receives an interaction or the author triggers channel creation\n * - Bot creates a text channel + invite link, saves to listing\n */\n\nconst BOT_TOKEN = process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\nconst CLIENT_ID = process.env.DISCORD_CLIENT_ID || \"\";\nconst DISCORD_API = \"https://discord.com/api/v10\";\n\nexport function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0 && CLIENT_ID.length > 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate a Discord snowflake ID (numeric string, 17-20 digits).\n * Used to prevent path traversal in Discord API URL construction.\n */\nexport function isValidSnowflake(id: string): boolean {\n  return /^\\d{17,20}$/.test(id);\n}\n\n/**\n * Call the Discord REST API.\n */\nasync function callApi<T = unknown>(\n  endpoint: string,\n  method: string = \"GET\",\n  body?: Record<string, unknown>\n): Promise<{ ok: boolean; data?: T; error?: string }> {\n  try {\n    const res = await fetch(`${DISCORD_API}${endpoint}`, {\n      method,\n      headers: {\n        Authorization: `Bot ${BOT_TOKEN}`,\n        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n      },\n      body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,\n    });\n\n    if (!res.ok) {\n      const errData = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));\n      return {\n        ok: false,\n        error: (errData as Record<string, string>).message || `HTTP ${res.status}`,\n      };\n    }\n\n    const data = (await res.json()) as T;\n    return { ok: true, data };\n  } catch (err) {\n    return { ok: false, error: String(err) };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get bot user info.\n */\nexport async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n  id: string;\n  username: string;\n} | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; username: string }>(\"/users/@me\");\n  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate a bot invite URL that includes the listing ID in the state parameter.\n * The bot needs permissions: Manage Channels, Create Instant Invite, Send Messages\n *\n * Permission bits:\n * - MANAGE_CHANNELS (0x10) = 16\n * - CREATE_INSTANT_INVITE (0x1) = 1\n * - SEND_MESSAGES (0x800) = 2048\n * - VIEW_CHANNEL (0x400) = 1024\n * Total: 3089\n */\nexport function generateBotInviteUrl(listingId: number): string {\n  const permissions = 3089;\n  const state = `listing_${listingId}`;\n  return `https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=${CLIENT_ID}&permissions=${permissions}&scope=bot&state=${encodeURIComponent(state)}`;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a text channel in a guild.\n */\nexport async function createTextChannel(\n  guildId: string,\n  name: string,\n  topic?: string\n): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n    `/guilds/${guildId}/channels`,\n    \"POST\",\n    {\n      name: name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, \"-\").slice(0, 100),\n      type: 0, // GUILD_TEXT\n      topic: topic || \"Bookmate reading group discussion channel\",\n    }\n  );\n  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create an invite for a channel.\n */\nexport async function createChannelInvite(\n  channelId: string,\n  maxAge: number = 0 // 0 = never expires\n): Promise<string | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ code: string }>(\n    `/channels/${channelId}/invites`,\n    \"POST\",\n    {\n      max_age: maxAge,\n      max_uses: 0,\n      unique: true,\n    }\n  );\n  if (res.ok && res.data) {\n    return `https://discord.gg/${res.data.code}`;\n  }\n  return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Send a message to a Discord channel.\n */\nexport async function sendChannelMessage(\n  channelId: string,\n  content: string\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  const res = await callApi(\n    `/channels/${channelId}/messages`,\n    \"POST\",\n    { content }\n  );\n  return res.ok;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get guild (server) info.\n */\nexport async function getGuild(\n  guildId: string\n): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n    `/guilds/${guildId}`\n  );\n  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n}\n\n/**\n * List guilds the bot is a member of.\n */\nexport async function getBotGuilds(): Promise<\n  { id: string; name: string }[]\n> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }[]>(\n    \"/users/@me/guilds\"\n  );\n  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : [];\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse listing ID from a state or payload string.\n * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n */\nexport function parseListingIdFromState(state: string): number | null {\n  const match = state.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n}\n","numLines":182,"startLine":1,"totalLines":182}}}

{ getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\nimport { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n\nexport interface SessionData {\n  userId?: number;\n  email?: string;\n  displayName?: string;\n}\n\nconst DEV_SECRET =\n  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n\nfunction getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    throw new Error(\n      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    );\n  }\n  return {\n    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    cookieOptions: {\n      httpOnly: true,\n      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n      sameSite: \"lax\",\n      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    },\n  };\n}\n\nexport async function getSession() {\n  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n}\n\nexport async function requireAuth() {\n  const session = await getSession();\n  if (!session.userId) {\n    return null;\n  }\n  return session;\n}\n","numLines":44,"startLine":1,"totalLines":44}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QUG7cgkcitQteek6nKujyp","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import nodemailer from \"nodemailer\";\n     2→\n     3→const isConfigured =\n     4→  !!process.env.SMTP_HOST &&\n     5→  !!process.env.SMTP_PORT &&\n     6→  !!process.env.SMTP_FROM;\n     7→\n     8→let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n     9→\n    10→function getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n    11→  if (!isConfigured) return null;\n    12→  if (!transporter) {\n    13→    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n    14→      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n    15→      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n    16→      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n    17→      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n    18→        ? {\n    19→            auth: {\n    20→              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n    21→              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n    22→            },\n    23→          }\n    24→        : {}),\n    25→    });\n    26→  }\n    27→  return transporter;\n    28→}\n    29→\n    30→export async function sendEmail(\n    31→  to: string,\n    32→  subject: string,\n    33→  text: string,\n    34→  html?: string\n    35→): Promise<boolean> {\n    36→  const t = getTransporter();\n    37→  if (!t) return false;\n    38→\n    39→  try {\n    40→    await t.sendMail({\n    41→      from: process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n    42→      to,\n    43→      subject,\n    44→      text,\n    45→      html: html || text,\n    46→    });\n    47→    return true;\n    48→  } catch (err) {\n    49→    console.error(\"Failed to send email:\", err);\n    50→    return false;\n    51→  }\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→export function isEmailConfigured(): boolean {\n    55→  return isConfigured;\n    56→}\n    57→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"9ee23102-3ffb-4e30-b767-37d178c08dd8","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:03:41.255Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/email.ts","content":"import nodemailer from \"nodemailer\";\n\nconst isConfigured =\n  !!process.env.SMTP_HOST &&\n  !!process.env.SMTP_PORT &&\n  !!process.env.SMTP_FROM;\n\nlet transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n\nfunction getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n  if (!isConfigured) return null;\n  if (!transporter) {\n    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n        ? {\n            auth: {\n              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n            },\n          }\n        : {}),\n    });\n  }\n  return transporter;\n}\n\nexport async function sendEmail(\n  to: string,\n  subject: string,\n  text: string,\n  html?: string\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  const t = getTransporter();\n  if (!t) return false;\n\n  try {\n    await t.sendMail({\n      from: process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n      to,\n      subject,\n      text,\n      html: html || text,\n    });\n    return true;\n  } catch (err) {\n    console.error(\"Failed to send email:\", err);\n    return false;\n  }\n}\n\nexport function isEmailConfigured(): boolean {\n  return isConfigured;\n}\n","numLines":57,"startLine":1,"totalLines":57}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018PcyNqbKdmQot5GQvetWFN","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→import { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n     3→import { escapeHtmlForEmail } from \"./crypto-utils\";\n     4→\n     5→function getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n     6→  const user = db\n     7→    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n     8→    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n     9→  return user?.email || null;\n    10→}\n    11→\n    12→export function createNotification(\n    13→  userId: number,\n    14→  listingId: number | null,\n    15→  type: string,\n    16→  message: string\n    17→) {\n    18→  db.prepare(\n    19→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    20→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    21→\n    22→  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n    23→  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n    24→  if (email) {\n    25→    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    26→    // HTML-escape the message to prevent injection via user-controlled content\n    27→    // (display names, book titles) embedded in notification messages\n    28→    const htmlBody = `<p>${escapeHtmlForEmail(message)}</p>`;\n    29→    sendEmail(email, subject, message, htmlBody).catch(() => {\n    30→      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    31→    });\n    32→  }\n    33→}\n    34→\n    35→function getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n    36→  switch (type) {\n    37→    case \"new_member\":\n    38→      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    39→    case \"group_full\":\n    40→      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    41→    case \"application_received\":\n    42→      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    43→    case \"application_approved\":\n    44→      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    45→    case \"application_rejected\":\n    46→      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    47→    case \"rating_received\":\n    48→      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    49→    case \"listing_deleted\":\n    50→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been cancelled\";\n    51→    case \"listing_updated\":\n    52→      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been updated\";\n    53→    case \"telegram_ready\":\n    54→      return \"Bookmate: Your Telegram reading group is ready!\";\n    55→    case \"discord_ready\":\n    56→      return \"Bookmate: Your Discord channel is ready!\";\n    57→    default:\n    58→      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    59→  }\n    60→}\n    61→\n    62→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    63→  listingId: number,\n    64→  joinerName: string\n    65→) {\n    66→  const listing = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    69→    )\n    70→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    71→\n    72→  if (listing) {\n    73→    createNotification(\n    74→      listing.author_id,\n    75→      listingId,\n    76→      \"new_member\",\n    77→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    78→    );\n    79→  }\n    80→}\n    81→\n    82→export function notifyApplicationReceived(\n    83→  listingId: number,\n    84→  applicantName: string\n    85→) {\n    86→  const listing = db\n    87→    .prepare(\n    88→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    89→    )\n    90→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    91→\n    92→  if (listing) {\n    93→    createNotification(\n    94→      listing.author_id,\n    95→      listingId,\n    96→      \"application_received\",\n    97→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    98→    );\n    99→  }\n   100→}\n   101→\n   102→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n   103→  userId: number,\n   104→  listingId: number,\n   105→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n   106→) {\n   107→  const listing = db\n   108→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   109→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   110→\n   111→  if (listing) {\n   112→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   113→    const message =\n   114→      decision === \"approved\"\n   115→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   116→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   117→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   118→  }\n   119→}\n   120→\n   121→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   122→  const listing = db\n   123→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference, author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   124→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string; platform_preference: string; author_id: number } | undefined;\n   125→  // Include all members (including the author) — author needs to know group is full to create the chat\n   126→  const members = db\n   127→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   128→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   129→\n   130→  if (listing) {\n   131→    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n   132→    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n   133→      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n   134→      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   135→    const message = botConfigured\n   136→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n   137→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n   138→\n   139→    for (const member of members) {\n   140→      createNotification(\n   141→        member.user_id,\n   142→        listingId,\n   143→        \"group_full\",\n   144→        message\n   145→      );\n   146→    }\n   147→  }\n   148→}\n   149→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"765b3981-b145-4ec4-b794-cf5ea2d79bd5","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:03:41.471Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts","content":"import db from \"./db\";\nimport { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\nimport { escapeHtmlForEmail } from \"./crypto-utils\";\n\nfunction getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n  const user = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n  return user?.email || null;\n}\n\nexport function createNotification(\n  userId: number,\n  listingId: number | null,\n  type: string,\n  message: string\n) {\n  db.prepare(\n    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n\n  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n  if (email) {\n    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    // HTML-escape the message to prevent injection via user-controlled content\n    // (display names, book titles) embedded in notification messages\n    const htmlBody = `<p>${escapeHtmlForEmail(message)}</p>`;\n    sendEmail(email, subject, message, htmlBody).catch(() => {\n      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    });\n  }\n}\n\nfunction getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n  switch (type) {\n    case \"new_member\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    case \"group_full\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    case \"application_received\":\n      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    case \"application_approved\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    case \"application_rejected\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    case \"rating_received\":\n      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    case \"listing_deleted\":\n      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been cancelled\";\n    case \"listing_updated\":\n      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been updated\";\n    case \"telegram_ready\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your Telegram reading group is ready!\";\n    case \"discord_ready\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your Discord channel is ready!\";\n    default:\n      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyListingAuthor(\n  listingId: number,\n  joinerName: string\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    createNotification(\n      listing.author_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"new_member\",\n      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyApplicationReceived(\n  listingId: number,\n  applicantName: string\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    createNotification(\n      listing.author_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"application_received\",\n      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyApplicationDecision(\n  userId: number,\n  listingId: number,\n  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n    const message =\n      decision === \"approved\"\n        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference, author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string; platform_preference: string; author_id: number } | undefined;\n  // Include all members (including the author) — author needs to know group is full to create the chat\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  if (listing) {\n    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n    const message = botConfigured\n      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n\n    for (const member of members) {\n      createNotification(\n        member.user_id,\n        listingId,\n        \"group_full\",\n        message\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n","numLines":149,"startLine":1,"totalLines":149}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KdQWqRzNsuJwuUWa6aGUyp","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  const { displayName, bio } = await req.json();\n    12→\n    13→  if (!displayName || displayName.trim().length === 0) {\n    14→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Display name is required\" }, { status: 400 });\n    15→  }\n    16→\n    17→  if (displayName.length > 100) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Display name must be at most 100 characters\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  if (bio && bio.length > 500) {\n    22→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Bio must be at most 500 characters\" }, { status: 400 });\n    23→  }\n    24→\n    25→  db.prepare(\"UPDATE users SET display_name = ?, bio = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n    26→    displayName.trim(),\n    27→    (bio || \"\").trim(),\n    28→    session.userId\n    29→  );\n    30→\n    31→  // Update session\n    32→  session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n    33→  await session.save();\n    34→\n    35→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    36→}\n    37→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c08cf456-6739-4bbc-8bb4-31628498e34f","uuid":"adb46a90-e3b8-433a-855c-aace73a2dff9","timestamp":"2026-03-24T11:03:41.698Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nexport async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { displayName, bio } = await req.json();\n\n  if (!displayName || displayName.trim().length === 0) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Display name is required\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  if (displayName.length > 100) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Display name must be at most 100 characters\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  if (bio && bio.length > 500) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Bio must be at most 500 characters\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  db.prepare(\"UPDATE users SET display_name = ?, bio = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n    displayName.trim(),\n    (bio || \"\").trim(),\n    session.userId\n  );\n\n  // Update session\n  session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n  await session.save();\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n","numLines":37,"startLine":1,"totalLines":37}}}
